verdict: A rhythmically accurate and well-paced chart with great energy tracking, marred by highly awkward hold-shielded patterns that force impossible one-foot runs and brackets.

issues.problem: Holding R while tapping L then D on 16th notes forces the player to perform an extremely fast and uncomfortable one-foot transition (L to D) in under 140ms.
A JUMP DR is placed while holding U, resulting in an unexpected 3-panel hand or bracket requirement that feels out of place on a Hard difficulty chart.
While holding U, the chart features a 16th-note stream of L -> R -> D, forcing the player's single free foot to clumsily rush across three distinct panels in 270ms.
